architecture_fit.note: Single multi-head attention block, no MLP, no positional encoding — a minimal delta that directly realizes the equivariance the goal asks about.

baseline_comparison.note: Only the supplied linear baseline (also 0.0); no failing strawman (e.g. positional bias) is shown to break equivariance, so there is no testable contrast.

faithfulness.note: Equivariance is measured directly on the model's outputs (A(PX) vs P·A·Pᵀ → 0), but no ablation and README does not address causal evidence.

operating_range.note: Runs only the canonical config (seq_len=8, one seed); no sweep over scale/length, and all-zero results give no breakage curve.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Mechanism is fully hand-built with fixed random weights (untrained), so equivariance is reproduced by construction rather than learned.

visual_judgement.note: Log-scale bar charts of strictly-zero errors render uninformatively, and the dropdown starts empty so plots are blank until the grader manually selects a run.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies the three views and ties them to the claim, but the log-scale-on-zeros choice undercuts legibility of the headline result.

notes: A correct, minimal attention block that demonstrates exact permutation equivariance (all Frobenius errors 0, rowsum dev ~1e-7), which is architecturally sound and on-question. However the experiment is thin: there is no failing strawman (e.g. a positional bias) to make the claim contrastive, no causal ablation, and only the canonical config is tested, so the all-zeros result is correct but unilluminating. Visualisation compounds this by plotting strictly-zero values on a log scale.
